name: GitHub
description: Talk to this agent by @mentioning it in GitHub issue and pull-request comments — a GitHub App with webhook signature verification via eve's githubChannel.
setup:
This channel makes the agent a **GitHub App** — its `@mention` identity in issues and pull requests, and the credential it uses to work the repositories you install it on. Each agent needs its own App: two agents sharing a slug can't both hear their mentions.

**Guided (recommended):** after installing this channel, open the agent's **Deployment** tab and click **Create GitHub App**. Eden registers the App on GitHub, stores its four secrets on the agent, and sends you to pick the repositories it watches. You can create it under your personal account or an organization you own.

**Manual:**

1. Create a **GitHub App** (Settings → Developer settings → GitHub Apps). Note its **App ID** → `GITHUB_APP_ID` and slug → `GITHUB_APP_SLUG` (the `@name` people mention).
2. Generate a **private key** (PEM) into `GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY`, and set a **webhook secret** → `GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
3. Deploy the agent, then set the App's **Webhook URL** to `https://<your-domain>/e/<environmentId>/eve/v1/github` (shown on the Deployment tab).
4. Subscribe to **Issue comment** and **Pull request review comment** events. Grant **Issues** and **Pull requests** read & write, and **Contents** read & write so the agent can clone, branch, and push.
5. Install the App on the repositories it should watch.

An issue or PR comment that `@mention`s the App's slug starts a turn.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_ID
secrets.description: The GitHub App's numeric App ID. Set automatically by Create GitHub App on the Deployment tab.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY
secrets.description: The GitHub App's private key (full PEM contents) — authenticates as the app to post replies and mint short-lived installation tokens for doing work. Set automatically by Create GitHub App.

secrets.name: GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET
secrets.description: The webhook secret configured on the App — eve verifies the inbound webhook signature against it. Set automatically by Create GitHub App.

secrets.name: GITHUB_APP_SLUG
secrets.description: The App's slug — the @name mentioned to trigger the agent. Set automatically by Create GitHub App (GitHub derives it from the final App name).
